'''Niall Evalds''' (born 26 August 1993) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|fb}} or {{rlp|wg}}er for the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]], and the [[England Knights]] and [[England national rugby league team|England]] at international level.

He has previously played for the [[Salford Red Devils]] and the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]]. He has spent time on loan from Salford at [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] in the [[2013 Championship 1|Championship 1]] and at the [[Barrow Raiders]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and on dual registration from Salford at [[Halifax RLFC|Halifax]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

Evalds has received one cap for England, and represented the England Knights twice. He holds the Salford club record for most Super League tries.<ref>{{Cite web |last1=Dainton |first1=Fraser |last2=Bazeley |first2=Marc |date=2021-02-10 |title=Niall Evalds: Salford's record Super League try-scorer on his new challenge at Castleford |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12214497/niall-evalds-salfords-record-super-league-try-scorer-on-his-new-challenge-at-castleford |access-date=2022-03-09 |website=Sky Sports}}</ref> He was awarded the [[Lance Todd Trophy]] in the [[2021 Challenge Cup final]].

==Early life==
Evalds was born in [[Halifax, West Yorkshire]], England. He is of [[Latvia]]n descent through his paternal grandfather Jānis ("John"), who settled in Halifax during World War II.{{citation needed|date=January 2020}}

==Career==
=== Salford Red Devils ===
Evalds made his Super League debut on 9 February 2013 against the [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/super-league-xviii-2013/round-2/catalans-vs-salford/summary.html|title=Catalans 40-6 Salford|website=www.rugbyleagueproject.org|access-date=13 Dec 2020}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2013-02-09 |title=Catalan Dragons 40-6 Salford |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/21325125 |access-date=2022-03-09 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>

He became [[Salford Red Devils]] leading [[Super League]] try scorer when he scored his 66th try in the competition against [[London Broncos]] in 2019.{{citation needed|date=January 2020}}

He played in the [[2019 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="St Helens give Justin Holbrook the perfect send-off with commanding Grand Final victory over Salford">{{cite news|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2019/10/12/st-helens-vs-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens give Justin Holbrook the perfect send-off with commanding Grand Final victory over Salford|newspaper=Telegraph|date=12 October 2019|access-date=25 October 2019|last1=Heppenstall|first1=Ross|last2=Bagchi|first2=Rob}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2019/oct/12/st-helens-v-salford-red-devils-super-league-grand-final-live/|title=St Helens 23-6 Salford Red Devils: Super League Grand Final – as it happened|website=The Guardian|date=12 October 2019|last1=Unwin|first1=Will}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/11777123/st-helens-win-2019-super-league-league-leaders-shield/|title=St Helens win 2019 Super League League Leaders' shield|website=Sky Sports}}</ref>

In May 2020, Salford Director Paul King stated that Evalds had rejected the opportunity to extend his contract with the Red Devils into 2021.

On 17 October 2020, he played for Salford in the [[2020 Challenge Cup Final]] which Salford lost 17–16 against the [[Leeds Rhinos]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/54555957|title=Leeds beat Salford 17-16 after Luke Gale lands late winning drop-goal|work=BBC Sport}}</ref>

=== Castleford Tigers ===
In August 2020, shortly after Super League's [[Super League XXV#Season resumption|resumption]] following its [[COVID-19 pandemic in the United Kingdom|COVID-19]] suspension, it was announced that Evalds had signed a one-year contract with the [[Castleford Tigers]] for the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/53967131|title=Niall Evalds: Castleford Tigers sign full-back from Salford Red Devils|work=BBC Sport|access-date=13 Dec 2020}}</ref> He said of the move, "This was an opportunity to take up a new challenge at Cas and have to prove myself all over again."<ref name="Sky Cas Signing">{{Cite web |date=2020-08-30 |title=Niall Evalds signs for Castleford Tigers for 2021 season from Salford Red Devils |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/13995/12059955/niall-evalds-signs-for-castleford-tigers-for-2021-season-from-salford-red-devils |access-date=2022-03-09 |website=Sky Sports}}</ref> Tigers head coach [[Daryl Powell]] described Evalds as "a high-quality player with a real instinct for rugby league".<ref name="Sky Cas Signing" />

Evalds made his Castleford debut and scored his first try for the club on 28 March 2021 against the [[Warrington Wolves]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-03-28 |title=Super League: Castleford 21-12 Warrington - Daryl Powell's Tigers too good for Wire |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/56517464 |access-date=2022-03-09 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> In May 2021, he signed a two-year contract extension with the Tigers, with the option of an additional year.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-05-12 |title=Niall Evalds: Castleford Tigers full-back extends contract |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57091587 |access-date=2022-03-09 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> On 17 July 2021, Evalds appeared in his second successive [[2021 Challenge Cup Final|Challenge Cup final]] as Castleford faced [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Scott |first=Ged |date=2021-07-17 |title=Challenge Cup: St Helens beat Castleford 26-12 for first Wembley victory since 2008 |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57828390 |access-date=2022-03-09 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He scored a try for Castleford and was voted winner of the [[Lance Todd Trophy]] for player of the match, despite the Tigers falling to a 12–26 defeat.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Craven |first1=Dave |title=Lance Todd Trophy winner Niall Evalds left "heartbroken" by second Challenge Cup final loss |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/lance-todd-trophy-winner-niall-evalds-left-heartbroken-by-second-challenge-cup-final-loss-3312053 |access-date=17 July 2021 |work=Yorkshire Post |date=17 July 2021}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-celebrate-challenge-cup-21081897/|title=How St Helens players have celebrated their first Challenge Cup win in 13 years|website=www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk|access-date=18 July 2021|archive-date=18 July 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210718102540/https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-celebrate-challenge-cup-21081897|url-status=dead}}</ref> He became the fourth Castleford player, and tenth from a losing side, to claim the award.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Bazeley |first=Marc |date=20 July 2021 |title=Niall Evalds: Bittersweet emotions for Castleford Tigers' Lance Todd Trophy winner after Challenge Cup final loss |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12358987/niall-evalds-bittersweet-emotions-for-castleford-tigers-lance-todd-trophy-winner-after-challenge-cup-final-loss |access-date=12 September 2023 |website=Sky Sports}}</ref> He finished the season having made 20 appearances and scored 7 tries.

Castleford's new head coach for the [[2022 Castleford Tigers season|2022 season]], [[Lee Radford]], was impressed by the ability of Evalds; he said, "I had him amongst the elite fullbacks in the competition, but he has probably surpassed that for me."<ref>{{Cite web |last=Thomson |first=Doug |date=2022-01-30 |title=Why Castleford Tigers' Niall Evalds is surpassing Lee Radford's expectations |url=https://www.totalrl.com/evalds-delights-his-coach/ |access-date=2022-03-09 |website=TotalRL}}</ref> A hamstring injury limited Evalds' playtime early in the year, and a ruptured bicep suffered in April ruled him out until July.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Ibbetson |first=Stephen |date=2022-04-26 |title=Castleford Tigers boss puts timescale on Niall Evalds injury blow |url=https://www.totalrl.com/castleford-tigers-boss-puts-timescale-on-niall-evalds-injury-blow/ |access-date=2022-07-23 |website=TotalRL}}</ref> On his second game back, he sustained a shoulder injury in the process of scoring a try against [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]], which ended his season prematurely.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Ibbetson |first=Stephen |date=2022-07-21 |title=Castleford Tigers fullback Niall Evalds expected to miss rest of season |url=https://www.totalrl.com/castleford-tigers-fullback-niall-evalds-expected-to-miss-rest-of-season/ |access-date=2022-07-23 |website=TotalRL}}</ref>

In March 2023, Evalds suffered another "freak injury" to his other shoulder. He said, "I want to play every game. Before the last year that's what I used to do."<ref>{{Cite web |last=Smith |first=Peter |date=12 April 2023 |title=Castleford Tigers' Niall Evalds talks 'freak injuries', team momentum and facing old side Salford Red Devils |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-niall-evalds-talks-freak-injuries-team-momentum-and-facing-old-side-salford-red-devils-4101487 |access-date=12 September 2023 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post}}</ref> He made a try-saving tackle on [[Joe Burgess]] on his return against [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] in round 9.<ref>{{Cite web |date=14 April 2023 |title=Salford Red Devils 14-6 Castleford Tigers: Play-off chasing hosts battle past Super League strugglers |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/salford-vs-castleford/58301 |access-date=12 September 2023 |website=Sky Sports}}</ref> In May, he suffered yet another injury – he underwent surgery on a pectoral tear, which brought an end to his season after just 7 appearances.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=25 May 2023 |title=Castleford suffer huge injury blow to full-back Niall Evalds |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/niall-evalds-castleford-injury-blow |access-date=12 September 2023 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> The Tigers later decided against offering a contract extension, with head coach [[Andy Last]] explaining, "We're not in a position at this moment to make him an offer because he's not playing. We need to make sure we cut our cloth accordingly and spend our budget."<ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=8 June 2023 |title=Hull KR target Niall Evalds set to leave Castleford Tigers, confirms Andy Last |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-kr-target-niall-evalds-set-to-leave-castleford-tigers-confirms-andy-last-4174519 |access-date=12 September 2023 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Shaw |first=Matthew |date=8 June 2023 |title=Hull KR cleared to sign Niall Evalds as Castleford Tigers boss denies interest in FC talent |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-kr-cleared-sign-niall-8505421 |access-date=12 September 2023 |website=Hull Daily Mail}}</ref> In August 2023, Castleford announced that Evalds would depart the club at the end of the season. He said, "I want to thank everyone at Cas, the fans, staff, and my teammates. They have all supported me through the tough times these last couple of years and I leave with lots of good memories at Cas."<ref>{{Cite web |date=23 August 2023 |title=Evalds to depart the Fords |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8735 |access-date=12 September 2023 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref>

=== Hull Kingston Rovers ===
On 23 August 2023, it was announced that Evalds would join [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] on a two-year deal from the 2024 season.<ref>{{Cite web |date=23 August 2023 |title=Niall Evalds: Hull KR to sign Castleford full-back from 2024 season on two-year deal |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66598924 |access-date=12 September 2023 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Darbyshire |first=Drew |date=23 August 2023 |title=Hull KR swoop for Castleford Tigers speedster with utility value earmarked |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/hull-kr-swoop-for-castleford-tigers-speedster-niall-evalds |access-date=12 September 2023 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref>
Evalds made a try-scoring debut against arch-rivals [[Hull FC]] in round 1 of the [[2024 Super League season]], scoring twice in a 22-0 victory.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68304214|title=Hull FC 0-22 Hull KR - Rovers win season opener as hosts have two sent off|website=BBC Sport}}</ref>
On 12 October 2024, Evalds played in the [[2024 Super League Grand Final]] loss against Wigan.<ref>{{cite news | url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/articles/cvgdzjx0l1ko | title=Wigan beat Hull KR in Grand Final to seal quadruple | first=Jay | last=Freeman | website=BBC Sport | date=12 October 2024 | access-date=17 March 2025}}</ref>

===Huddersfield Giants (loan)===
On 17 March 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]] on loan for the remainder of the 2025 season, and then becoming a permanent move from 2026.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=17 Mar 2025|author=Aaron Bower|title=Niall Evalds’ Hull KR exit confirmed as fullback breaks silence on move|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/niall-evalds-hull-kr-exit-confirmed-as-fullback-breaks-silence-on-move}}</ref><ref>{{cite news | url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/articles/cjrypdy5ze4o | title=Huddersfield sign Hull KR full-back Evalds on loan | date=17 March 2025 | access-date=17 March 2025 | website=BBC Sport}}</ref> Evalds made his debut at former club [[Salford Red Devils]] but only lasted 40 minutes before he was forced off with a [[metatarsal]] injury keeping him sidelined for several weeks.

On 12 August 2025 it was confirmed that his loan move will become permanent for the 2026, as he signed a 2-year deal with [[Huddersfield Giants]]<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=12 Aug 2025|accessdate=27 Sep 2025|title=Huddersfield sign Evalds permanently from Hull KR|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cj3lyrjjyldo}}</ref>

==International career==
In July 2018 he was selected in the [[England Knights]] Performance squad.<ref name="Ten new additions in England Knights squad">{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ten-new-additions-in-england-knights-squad/|title=Ten new additions in England Knights squad|publisher=loverugbyleague|date=24 July 2018|access-date=31 October 2019}}</ref> Later that year he was selected for the England Knights on their tour of [[Papua New Guinea]]. He played in their 12–16 victory against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] at the Lae Football Stadium in [[Lae]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2018-10-23 |title=England Knights squad to face PNG in first Test |url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/53841/england-knights-squad-to-face-png-in-first-test |access-date=2022-03-09 |website=Rugby-League.com}}</ref><ref name="Papua New Guinea vs England Knights">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/papua-new-guinea-v-england-knights-international-2018/718006#lineup|title=Papua New Guinea vs England Knights|publisher=WWOS|date=27 October 2018|access-date=30 October 2019}}</ref>

In 2019 he was selected for the England Knights in their 38–6 victory against [[Jamaica national rugby league team|Jamaica]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Smith |first=Peter |date=2019-10-20 |title=England Knights 38 Jamaica 6: Minikin hat-trick secures Knights win |url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/england-knights-38-jamaica-6-minikin-hat-trick-scures-knights-win-817159 |access-date=2022-03-09 |website=Yorkshire Evening Post}}</ref><ref name="England Knights vs Jamaica">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/england-knights-v-jamaica-international-2019/719012#lineup|title=England Knights vs Jamaica|publisher=WWOS|date=20 October 2019|access-date=30 October 2019}}</ref>

Evalds earned his first senior [[England national rugby league team|England]] cap in their 30–10 victory over [[France national rugby league team|France]] at the [[Stade Gilbert Brutus]] on 23 October 2021.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Hafez |first=Shamoon |date=2021-10-23 |title=France 10-30 England: Bateman, Farrell, Davies, Makinson with tries |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/59022672 |access-date=2022-03-09 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2021-10-23 |title=France vs England |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/france-vs-england/teams/55799 |access-date=2022-03-09 |website=Sky Sports}}</ref>
